channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Police Inquiry Stakes and the Path to Safer South Africa
item.description: The article discusses the ongoing impact of the Madlanga commission of inquiry, established a year after KwaZulu-Natal police commissioner Lt-Gen Nhlanhla Mkhwanazi's explosive allegations of politica...

item.title: Home Sodium Batteries for Energy Storage: A New Approach to Managing Electricity Costs
item.description: The article explores the potential of domestic sodium batteries as a solution for optimizing electricity costs in residential settings. It explains how these batteries could be used to store energy du...

item.title: Eight arrested in ₹12 crore sugar factory fraud case
item.description: The District Police in Vijayapura have arrested eight individuals involved in a prolonged fraud scheme at a sugar factory, defrauding farmers and the factory itself of ₹12 crore over six years. The in...

item.title: Ethiopia's IMF funds arrive as inflation challenges central bank's control
item.description: Ethiopia has received a $464 million disbursement from the International Monetary Fund (IMF), marking the completion of the country's fifth review. This financial support is critical as the National B...

item.title: Concerns Rise Over Mitch McConnell's Health and Elaine Chao's Unusual Diplomatic Activities
item.description: Prominent MAGA activist Kylie Jane Kremer has escalated concerns over Senator Mitch McConnell (R-KY) following his recent hospitalization after being found unconscious. Kremer, associated with Women f...

item.title: Pakistan Army Committed to Ensuring Water Rights Amid India Disputes
item.description: The Pakistan Army has reaffirmed its commitment to securing the country's rightful share of water amid ongoing tensions with India, as highlighted in a statement released by the Inter-Services Public ...

item.title: FIFA President Reaffirms Judicial Independence Amid Trump's Inquiry on Balogun Suspension
item.description: FIFA President Gianni Infantino has confirmed that U.S. President Donald Trump contacted him regarding the one-match suspension of USA forward Folarin Balogun. However, Infantino emphasized that the c...

item.title: Climate Change Disrupts Bird Migration Patterns, Threatening Populations
item.description: Climate change is causing significant disruptions to bird migration patterns, with far-reaching consequences for ecosystems and biodiversity. A study by Professor Morgan Tingley of the University of C...

item.title: Canada's Services Economy Contracts in June Amid Geopolitical Uncertainty and Inflation Concerns
item.description: Canada's services economy contracted in June, marking its lowest level since February, according to S&P Global's PMI data. The Business Activity Index dropped to 47.1 from 50.6 in May, indicating a co...

item.title: Student Unrest Forces Closure of Kisii School Over New Principal Appointment
item.description: Nyabisase Mixed Senior School in Kisii County was forced to close after violent clashes erupted between students and teachers following the appointment of a new principal. The Teachers Service Commiss...

item.title: Albanese's Apology for Joke About Kylie Minogue Sparks Debate
item.description: Steve Evans' op-ed critiques Prime Minister Anthony Albanese's public apology for a joke about Kylie Minogue, arguing it was an overreaction to media sensationalism. The article highlights how the PM'...

item.title: Lauren Bennett, former GRL and LMFAO member, passes away at 37; bandmates mourn
item.description: Lauren Bennett, a former member of the girl group GRL and collaborator with LMFAO, has passed away at the age of 37. Her death was confirmed by her ex-bandmates in a heartfelt statement, expressing th...

item.title: FIFA President Clarifies No Trump Influence in Balogun Red Card Case
item.description: FIFA president Gianni Infantino has defended the independence of world football's judicial bodies after controversy erupted over overturning the suspension of US striker Folarin Balogun. He stressed t...

item.title: Ram Temple Trust Resignation and Mumbai Rain Deaths: Key Developments
item.description: The Ram temple trust in Ayodhya has accepted the resignations of its general secretary, Champat Rai, and trustee Anil Mishra, amid allegations of embezzlement of donations. The duo resigned on 'moral ...

item.title: Pakistan's Digital Payment Growth Surpasses 2 Million Merchants
item.description: The government of Pakistan has reported significant progress in its 'Cashless Pakistan' initiative, with active digital payment merchants surpassing 2 million. Minister of State for Railways and Finan...

item.title: Former Tanzanian President Kikwete Leads Commemoration of 50 Years of Britain-Tanzania Society Collaboration
item.description: Former Tanzanian President Jakaya Kikwete led commemorations for the 50th anniversary of the Britain-Tanzania Society, a key platform for bilateral cooperation between Tanzania and the UK. The event i...

item.title: ViaSat's Stock Rises on Wireless Spectrum Opportunities
item.description: The article discusses the recent surge in ViaSat's stock price, driven by investor enthusiasm over the company's potential to capitalize on wireless spectrum opportunities. ViaSat, a leading satellite...

item.title: Duterte's Defense Team Resists Arraignment in Impeachment Trial
item.description: The defense team of Vice President Sara Duterte has firmly opposed the arraignment during her impeachment trial, asserting that it is unnecessary. The Senate impeachment court began Duterte's trial on...

item.title: Madlanga Commission Continues Inquiry into Justice System Allegations
item.description: The Madlanga commission of inquiry, which is investigating allegations of criminality, political interference, and corruption within the criminal justice system, is proceeding with its hearings on Wed...

item.title: Bird Flu Crisis: A Growing Global Health Concern
item.description: The article discusses the ongoing threat of bird flu, highlighting its rapid spread among poultry populations and increasing concerns about potential human transmission. Experts warn that the virus is...
